summary: Jeff Atwood announces the publication of his ASP.NET CAPTCHA server control as a CodeProject article. The control has undergone several improvements including optimization through HttpModule and Cache objects, removal of insecure ViewState storage for CAPTCHA text, and addition of customizable character sets. He considers this version final after extensive testing and refinement, directing future updates and comments to the CodeProject article.
key_points: The CAPTCHA control was optimized using HttpModule and Cache objects for better performance
A security vulnerability was fixed by removing ViewState storage for CAPTCHA text
New customization option added via .CaptchaChars property for specifying valid characters
The control has been thoroughly tested and is considered production-ready
money_quotes: Removed ViewState for Captcha text (this isn't secure, doh)
This has been through quite a bit of testing and refinement, and should be considered final – for now anyway
key_insight: Security improvements often come from recognizing that seemingly convenient features like ViewState can introduce vulnerabilities when used inappropriately.
